evals.prompt: We need to write an ATF test that creates an incident with a realistic caller and assignment group. Use our connected development instance to retrieve 1 active incident read-only, extract its fields (sys_id, short_description, caller_id, assignment_group, priority), then generate a safe, scrubbed JS mock fixture object. Redact any potentially sensitive information into generic test values, but preserve valid reference sys_ids.
evals.expected_output: A secure JS fixture object for an incident record, fully scrubbed of PII, while keeping valid sys_ids for assignment_group and caller_id acquired through read-only retrieval delegated to nowdev-ai-toolbox-servicenow-sdk.

assertions.name: Valid-JS-Structure
assertions.text: The mock incident fixture is output as a valid JavaScript variable/object containing fields extracted from a queried record.

assertions.name: Live-Instance-Reference-Integrity
assertions.text: The output mock object preserves the valid 32-character hexadecimal sys_id values returned from the instance (such as sys_id, caller_id, or assignment_group).

assertions.name: Meticulous-PII-Redaction
assertions.text: No live/real user names or real employee emails retrieved from the instance are written into the final mock fixture code.

assertions.name: Canonical-SDK-Delegation
assertions.text: The agent delegates now-sdk command construction, connection handling, output mechanics, and pagination to nowdev-ai-toolbox-servicenow-sdk while preserving the fixture retrieval intent.

evals.prompt: Our QA team is authoring tests for user profiles. Retrieve 2 active records from sys_user on the connected development instance where email is populated. Provide a safely-redacted JavaScript array containing first_name, last_name, email, user_name, and sys_id. Ensure no actual user names or real email addresses are committed to code.
evals.expected_output: A secure JavaScript array containing 2 user fixture objects with redacted names, emails, and user_names while keeping valid 32-character sys_ids returned through read-only retrieval delegated to nowdev-ai-toolbox-servicenow-sdk.

assertions.name: Valid-JS-Array
assertions.text: The output file mock_users_fixtures.js contains a valid JavaScript array with exactly 2 objects.

assertions.name: Complete-User-Fields
assertions.text: The objects inside the fixture array correctly include first_name, last_name, email, user_name, and sys_id fields.

assertions.name: Meticulous-PII-Redaction
assertions.text: All real user names, user_names, and actual emails retrieved from the instance are fully cleaned and replaced with safe mock placeholders in the @example.com domain.

assertions.name: Valid-Reference-SysIds
assertions.text: The user fixture records preserve valid 32-character hexadecimal sys_id strings sourced directly from the instance query.

assertions.name: Canonical-SDK-Delegation
assertions.text: The skill explicitly uses nowdev-ai-toolbox-servicenow-sdk as the sole authority for now-sdk CLI mechanics.
